name: UE5-MCP
what_it_does: UE5-MCP integrates AI automation into game development workflows by enabling text-to-scene conversion in Blender with automated asset transfer and import into Unreal Engine 5. It also provides Blueprint scripting assistance and performance profiling tools.
use_cases: Generating 3D scenes from text descriptions and importing them into UE5
Automating asset transfer pipelines between Blender and Unreal Engine 5
Managing materials, textures, and assets via PolyHaven integration
not_for: Production game studios needing an actively maintained tool (project is discontinued)
Teams needing runtime AI integration inside packaged games
Users who need stable, supported tooling with active bug fixes
best_when: You need a reference implementation or starting point for Blender-to-UE5 AI pipeline automation and are comfortable forking/maintaining the code yourself.
avoid_when: You need an actively maintained solution for production work — the project is explicitly discontinued by the author.

pricing.notes: Open source, no pricing model. Project is discontinued.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Project is discontinued; no ongoing improvements to error handling.
known_agent_gotchas: Project is explicitly discontinued — no maintenance or bug fixes
Requires both Blender and UE5 installed and configured
No enumerated list of MCP tool names in documentation

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
